subject: Statistics
title: Trends: The Long-Term Direction
description: Learn Trends: The Long-Term Direction in one minute.

hook.text: Global temperatures rose 1.1°C over 140 years. That's the trend — the slow, persistent direction beneath the noise.

buildup.text: A trend is the long-term increase or decrease in a time series, stripped of short-term fluctuations.

discovery.text: Moving averages smooth out noise to reveal the trend. A 12-month moving average removes seasonal swings.

twist.text: Trends can be linear, exponential, or polynomial. Choosing the wrong shape leads to wildly different forecasts.

climax.text: Detecting a genuine trend versus a random drift is one of the hardest problems in time series analysis.

punchline.text: Zoom out far enough and the direction emerges. The trend is the story.

quiz.question: How do moving averages help reveal trends?
options: They smooth out short-term fluctuations, making the long-term direction visible
They amplify noise so patterns are easier to see
They only work for financial data
